attacking_style: Launches long-range, sustained attacks (60km documented) rather than explosive accelerations. Most effective in final 2-3km of HC climbs when rivals are already depleted. Post-2021, lost ability to accelerate decisively.
team_tactics: INEOS sets controlled tempo through multiple domestiques before his moves. Pre-injury willing to attack even with teammates ahead to test GC rivals. Post-injury receives full team support but cannot capitalize on positioning.
terrain_preference: High-altitude, sustained HC climbs where gradual pace favors his diesel engine. Vulnerable on punchy accelerations and explosive gradients. Descends aggressively when in form (2021 Vuelta 60km move).
vulnerability: Completely exposed on sustained climbing efforts post-2023 injury. Cannot follow even gradual threshold pace. Loses significant time on climbs that previously suited him (Marie Blanc, Catalunya). Short explosive accelerations problematic even in peak form.
key_patterns: Long-range attacks 60km+ out when in form, not final-kilometer explosions (2021 Vuelta evidence).
Post-2023 injury shows persistent climbing weakness regardless of gradient or pace (Catalunya, TDF examples).
Will attack yellow jersey group even with teammates ahead to probe weaknesses (2019 TDF pattern).
Unable to respond to rival accelerations in final kilometers across multiple years (PINOT 2019, Roglic 2021).
Team provides maximum support but individual acceleration capacity missing even when positioned (2021 Vuelta pattern).
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 observations show continued severe climbing deficits post-injury with no recovery timeline evident. Lost on gradual and explosive efforts alike.
